parameters.content:
## Client Meeting Recap Email — Fireflies + GPT-4o-mini + Gmail + Sheets

For account managers, consultants, and agency owners who want a polished client recap email sent automatically after every meeting. When Fireflies finishes transcribing a meeting, this workflow detects the client email from participants, uses GPT-4o-mini to write a warm 250-word professional recap, sends it via Gmail with your reply-to address, and logs every sent recap to Google Sheets. The client receives a genuine-sounding email — no AI footprint, no Fireflies links, just a professional summary of what was discussed and what happens next.

## How it works
- **1. Webhook — Fireflies Transcript Done** receives the meetingId from Fireflies on transcription complete
- **2. Set — Config Values** stores Fireflies API key, sender details, company name, Sheet ID, and client email detection settings
- **3. HTTP — Fetch Transcript** calls the Fireflies GraphQL API for title, date, participants, transcript URL, overview, keywords, action items, gist, and bullet points
- **4. Code — Extract Data and Detect Client Email** detects the client email using three strategies: find a participant whose domain differs from yourEmailDomain, extract an email embedded in the meeting title, or fall back to defaultClientEmail
- **5. AI Agent — Write Recap Email** uses GPT-4o-mini (temperature 0.6) to write a warm professional recap with greeting, key points, action items, and sign-off
- **7. Code — Prepare Email Fields** reads the AI output and builds the email subject and all fields for sending and logging
- **8. Gmail — Send Recap Email** sends the recap to the detected client email with your address as reply-to
- **9. Google Sheets — Log Sent Recaps** appends one row per sent recap for tracking

## Set up steps
1. Activate the workflow and copy the webhook URL from node 1
2. In Fireflies — go to Settings, Developer Settings, Webhooks, and paste the URL
3. In **2. Set — Config Values** — replace all seven values: Fireflies API key, sender name, sender email, company name, Google Sheet ID, default client email, and your company domain
4. In **6. OpenAI — GPT-4o-mini Model** — connect your OpenAI credential
5. In **8. Gmail — Send Recap Email** — connect your Gmail OAuth2 credential using the account that matches senderEmail
6. In **9. Google Sheets — Log Sent Recaps** — connect your Google Sheets OAuth2 credential
7. Create a Google Sheet tab named Recap Email Log with columns: Date, Meeting Title, Client Email, Email Subject, Status, Fireflies URL, Logged At

nodes.name: Section — Webhook, Config, and Transcript Fetch

parameters.content:
## Webhook, Config, and Transcript Fetch
Fireflies POSTs a meetingId here on transcription complete. Config stores sender details, client email fallback, and your company domain for smart client detection. HTTP fetches the full Fireflies summary — no full sentences needed.

## Client Email Detection
Three-strategy detection: first looks for a participant whose email domain differs from yourEmailDomain, then checks if the meeting title contains an email, then falls back to defaultClientEmail. Also extracts client name from email format.

## AI Recap Email Writing
GPT-4o-mini (temperature 0.6) writes a warm 250-word professional recap. Structure: greeting, thank you opener, key points as bullets, action items, closing, sign-off. Plain text only — no AI mention, no Fireflies links.

nodes.name: Section — Email Assembly and Parallel Delivery

parameters.content:
## Email Assembly and Parallel Delivery
Builds email subject and assembles all fields. Gmail sends the recap to the client with your email as reply-to so replies come directly to you. Google Sheets logs every sent recap simultaneously.

nodes.name: 4. Code — Extract Data and Detect Client Email

parameters.text:
=You are a professional account manager at {{ $json.companyName }} writing a post-meeting recap email to a client.

MEETING DETAILS:
Meeting Title: {{ $json.meetingTitle }}
Date: {{ $json.meetingDate }}
Duration: {{ $json.durationMin }} minutes
Sent by: {{ $json.senderName }}, {{ $json.companyName }}
Client Name: {{ $json.clientName }}

FIREFLIES MEETING SUMMARY:
Gist: {{ $json.gist }}
Overview: {{ $json.overview }}
Key Points: {{ $json.bulletPoints }}
Topics Discussed: {{ $json.keywords }}

ACTION ITEMS FROM MEETING:
{{ $json.actionItems }}

Write a professional, warm recap email to send to the client after the meeting.

EMAIL STRUCTURE:

Line 1: Greeting using client name (Hi [name], or Dear [name],)

Paragraph 1 — Opening:
Thank the client for their time. Reference the meeting title or topic naturally. Keep it warm and genuine. 2 sentences maximum.

Paragraph 2 — What We Covered:
Summarize the key points discussed in 3 to 4 bullet points using plain dashes. Be specific — use real content from the meeting summary. Not generic filler.

Paragraph 3 — Next Steps:
List the action items clearly. Format each as: dash, action description, who is responsible. If no action items write one line: We will follow up shortly with next steps.

Paragraph 4 — Closing:
One warm closing sentence. Invite them to reach out with any questions.

Sign off:
Best regards,
[senderName]
[companyName]

RULES:
- Plain text only. No markdown. No asterisks. No hashtags.
- Professional but warm tone — not corporate or robotic.
- Maximum 250 words total.
- Do NOT mention that this was generated by AI.
- Do NOT reveal internal notes or Fireflies links.
- Write as if you personally attended the meeting and are writing this immediately after.

main.node: 4. Code — Extract Data and Detect Client Email
